name: Kerr County
description_short: Kerr County Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving seven Texas counties, offering savings accounts, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Kerr County Federal Credit Union (KCFCU) operates as a federally chartered credit union serving members across Kerr, Kimble, Edwards, Bandera, Kendall, Real, and Gillespie Counties in Texas. The institution expanded its field of membership to include persons who live, work, worship, or attend school in these counties, plus their immediate families and local businesses. This broader membership model allows the credit union to serve a larger geographic and demographic footprint than traditional single-county institutions.

KCFCU provides a comprehensive suite of financial services including checking and savings accounts, personal loans, auto loans, share-secured loans, and various other lending products. The credit union has invested in modern digital infrastructure, offering online banking accessible via computer or smartphone, a mobile app with remote deposit capture, bill pay functionality, e-statements, and debit card management features. Members can access their accounts 24/7 through these digital channels, check balances, make transfers between accounts, schedule payments, and view cleared check images.

What distinguishes KCFCU is its cooperative structure and community focus as a credit union rather than a traditional bank. The institution emphasizes member service with a dedicated loan department and actively recruits qualified community members to serve on its Supervisory Committee. The credit union maintains a routing number (314985345) and operates physical locations, though the website does not specify branch counts. The mobile app specifically includes features like check endorsement guidance and troubleshooting support for remote deposits, suggesting attention to member education.

As a regional credit union serving a modest-sized geographic area, KCFCU appears well-suited for members in its service territory seeking straightforward banking services and community-oriented lending. However, the website provides limited information about specific loan rates, account features, membership fees, or competitive advantages, making detailed comparison difficult. The institution does not prominently advertise specialized products like credit monitoring, identity theft protection, or premium accounts, suggesting a focus on foundational banking rather than comprehensive financial services.
services: Online banking with account balance checks, transfer capabilities, and payment scheduling
Mobile banking app with remote deposit capture and photo-based check deposits
Bill pay services with secure payment processing
Debit card freeze and unfreeze controls
Check image viewing for cleared transactions
Payment reminders and deposit balance alerts
pros: Expanded field of membership across seven Texas counties serves broader population than traditional county-based credit unions
Mobile app includes remote check deposit with photo guidance and troubleshooting support for member convenience
Comprehensive digital banking platform with online access, bill pay, e-statements, and debit card freeze/unfreeze controls
Dedicated loan department offering personal loans, auto loans, and share-secured loan products
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns institutional incentives with member financial success
Free e-statements option supports paperless, environmentally conscious banking
Bilingual website support (English/Spanish) increases accessibility for diverse membership
cons: Website provides no information about specific loan rates, terms, or APRs to enable rate shopping
No details on account fees, minimum balances, or account types available from website content
Limited transparency about physical branch locations or ATM network coverage despite mentioning ATM locator
No mention of NCUA insurance coverage limits or member protections on website
Sparse information about membership eligibility verification process or account opening requirements
best_for: Residents of Kerr, Kimble, Edwards, Bandera, Kendall, Real, or Gillespie Counties seeking community-based banking
Employees and business owners in the seven-county service area wanting member-owned financial institution benefits
Tech-savvy members seeking modern mobile banking and remote deposit capabilities from a credit union
Borrowers needing personal or auto loans from a local lender with direct loan department contact
diagnosis: Kerr County FCU is best for members living or working in its seven-county Texas service area who value community-based banking and modern digital convenience. The main caveat is the lack of transparent pricing information and limited detail about competitive advantages—prospective members must contact the credit union directly to compare loan rates, account features, and fees.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Kerr County — Credit Union in Kerrville, TX | CreditDoc
meta_description: Kerr County — NCUA-insured credit union in Kerrville, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
